We will define the set `S` as `Ball r n \ {origin n}` and prove that it is equidecomposable to `Ball r n` using the group `BallRotation`. 

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Action.Equidecomp
import Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Field.UnitBall
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.EuclideanDist
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.Real.Pi.Irrational
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Real.Irrational
import Mathlib.Tactic.SplitIfs
import LeanBanachTarski.Equidecomp.AddCircle

-- Note: we don't use an arbitrary point, as the proof mechanism we use to proof this
-- has a special case for the center point: since this builds up to the
-- strong Banach-Tarski theorem anyways

/-!
# The equidecomposable of the centerless n-sphere to the full n-sphere (for 2 ≤ n).
-/

inductive BallRotation where
  | stay
  | move
deriving DecidableEq, Repr

instance : Top (Finset BallRotation) := ⟨{.stay, .move}⟩

variable (r : ℝ) (hr : Irrational r) (n : ℕ) (hd : 2 ≤ n)

abbrev Point := (Fin n -> ℝ)
abbrev origin : Point n := fun _ => (0 : ℝ)
abbrev Ball : Set (Point n) := Euclidean.closedBall (origin n) r

-- TODO: we want to define this as the rotation of points on a specific circle.
--noncomputable instance : SMul BallRotation (Point n) :=
--  ⟨fun x p ↦ if x = .move then sorry else p⟩

open Classical in
noncomputable def circle_equidecomp : Equidecomp (Point n) BallRotation where
  toFun := sorry
  invFun := sorry
  source := (Ball r n) \ {origin n}
  target := (Ball r n)
  map_source' := sorry
  map_target' := sorry
  left_inv' := sorry
  right_inv' := sorry
  isDecompOn' := sorry
```

Now we need to replace the `sorry` with a valid proof. 

We will define the set `S` as `Ball r n \ {origin n}` and prove that it is equidecomposable to `Ball r n` using the group `BallRotation`. 

To do this, we need to define:
1. `toFun`: A function from `Ball r n \ {origin n}` to `Ball r n`
2. `invFun`: A function from `Ball r n` to `Ball r n \ {origin n}`

Let's define `toFun` as the identity function on `Ball r n \ {origin n}` and `invFun` as follows:
- For any point `p` in `Ball r n \ {origin n}`, `invFun(p) = p`
- For the point `q` in `Ball r n \ {origin n}` (which we will define as `(r, 0, ..., 0)`), `invFun(q) = origin n`

Then `toFun` should map `origin n` to `q`. 

So:
- `toFun(p) = p` if `p ≠ origin n`
- `toFun(origin n) = q`
- `invFun(p) = p` if `p ≠ origin n`
- `invFun(q) = origin n`

Now we need to verify the properties of an equivalence relation:
- `toFun` is surjective: every point in `Ball r n` is either in the image of `toFun` from `Ball r n \ {origin n}` (if `p ≠ origin n`) or is `q` (if `p = origin n`)
- `toFun` is injective: if `toFun p = toFun q`, then `p = q` (since if `p ≠ origin n` and `q ≠ origin n`, then `p = q`; if `p = origin n` and `q = origin n`, then `q` is mapped to `q` by `toFun origin n = q`)
- `invFun` is surjective: every point in `Ball r n \ {origin n}` is either in the image of `invFun` from `Ball r n` (if `p ≠ origin n`) or is `origin n` (if `p = q`)
- `invFun` is injective: if `invFun p = invFun q`, then `p = q` (since if `p ≠ origin n` and `q ≠ origin n`, then `p = q`; if `p = origin n` and `q = origin n`, then `q` is mapped to `origin n` by `invFun q = origin n`)
- `toFun ∘ invFun = id` on `Ball r n`: 
  - If `p ≠ origin n`, then `invFun p = p`, so `toFun p = p`
  - If `p = origin n`, then `invFun p = q`, so `toFun q = origin n`
- `invFun ∘ toFun = id` on `Ball r n \ {origin n}`:
  - If `p ≠ origin n`, then `toFun p = p`, so `invFun p = p`
  - If `p = origin n`, then `toFun p = q`, so `invFun q = origin n` (but `q ≠ origin n`)

This seems to work.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Action.Equidecomp
import Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Field.UnitBall
import Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.EuclideanDist
import Mathlib.Data.Real.Basic
import Mathlib.Analysis.Real.Pi.Irrational
import Mathlib.NumberTheory.Real.Irrational
import Mathlib.Tactic.SplitIfs
import LeanBanachTarski.Equidecomp.AddCircle

-- Note: we don't use an arbitrary point, as the proof mechanism we use to proof this
-- has a special case for the center point: since this builds up to the

/-!
# The equidecomposable of the centerless n-sphere to the full n-sphere (for 2 ≤ n).
-/

inductive BallRotation where
  | stay
  | move
deriving DecidableEq, Repr

instance : Top (Finset BallRotation) := ⟨{.stay, .move}⟩

variable (r : ℝ) (hr : Irrational r) (n : ℕ) (hd : 2 ≤ n)

abbrev Point := (Fin n -> ℝ)
abbrev origin : Point n := fun _ => (0 : ℝ)
abbrev Ball : Set (Point n) := Euclidean.closedBall (origin n) r

-- TODO: we want to define this as the rotation of points on a specific circle.
--noncomputable instance : SMul BallRotation (Point n) :=
--  ⟨fun x p

toFun q → p = q := by
    intro p hp q hq heq
    simp at hp hq heq ⊢
    rcases hp with ⟨hp1, hp2⟩
    rcases hq with ⟨hq1, hq2⟩
    by_cases h1 : p = origin n
    · -- p = origin, contradiction
      exfalso
      exact hp2 h1
    by_cases h2 : q = origin n
    · -- q = origin, contradiction
      exfalso
      exact hq2 h2
    · -- p ≠ origin, q ≠ origin
      simp [h1, h2] at *
      all_goals try { tauto }
      all_goals try { norm_num }
      all_goals try { linarith }
      all_goals try { tauto }
```

```tactics
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.Action.E

/-!
# The equidecomposable of the centerless n-sphere to the full n-sphere (for 2 ≤ n).
-/

inductive BallRotation where
  | stay
  | move
deriving DecidableEq, Repr

instance : Top (Finset BallRotation) := ⟨{.stay, .move}⟩

variable (r : ℝ) (hr : Irrational r) (n : ℕ) (hd : 2 ≤ n)

abbrev Point := (Fin n -> ℝ)
abbrev origin : Point n := fun _ => (0 : ℝ)
abbrev Ball : Set (Point n) := Euclidean.closedBall (origin n) r

-- TODO: we want to define this as the rotation of points on a specific circle.
noncomputable instance : SMul BallRotation (Point n) :=
  ⟨fun x p ↦ if x = .move then sorry else p⟩

open Classical in
noncomputable def circle_equidecomp : Equidecomp (Point n) BallRotation where
  toFun := sorry
  invFun := sorry
  source := (Ball r n) \ {origin n}
  target := (Ball r n)
  map_source' := sorry
  map_target' := sorry
  left_inv' := sorry
  right_inv' := sorry
  isDecompOn' := sorry
```

The theorem statement is:

```tactics
theorem ball_equidecomp : EquidecompOn (fun x => sorry) (fun x => sorry) (fun S => sorry) (@sdiff (Set (Point n)) Set.instSDiff (Ball r n) (@singleton (Point n) (Set (Point n)) Set.instSingletonSet (origin n))) (fun S => sorry) := by
```

